speaker: Amalekite Messenger
quote_text: I happened to be on Mount Gilboa
prompt_context: After Saul's death, a young man arrived from the camp and reported to David what he claimed to have done for the wounded king.
question: Who told David that he had been on Mount Gilboa and claimed to have killed Saul?

speaker: David
quote_text: How is it you were not afraid?
prompt_context: After hearing the report of Saul's death, David challenged the messenger who said he had struck the Lord's anointed.
question: Who asked the Amalekite messenger why he was not afraid to destroy the Lord's anointed?

speaker: David
quote_text: I am distressed for you
prompt_context: In his lament over Saul and Jonathan, David spoke tenderly of the fallen son of Saul.
question: Who lamented over Jonathan, saying he was distressed for him?

speaker: God
quote_text: Go up to Judah
prompt_context: After Saul's death, David inquired of the Lord about whether he should return to one of Judah's cities.
question: Who told David to go up to Judah after Saul's death?

speaker: David
quote_text: May you be blessed by the Lord
prompt_context: After hearing that the men of Jabesh-gilead had buried Saul, David sent them a message of gratitude and kindness.
question: Who blessed the men of Jabesh-gilead for burying Saul?

speaker: Abner
quote_text: Am I a dog's head?
prompt_context: After Ish-bosheth accused Abner concerning Rizpah, Abner angrily defended his loyalty to Saul's house.
question: Who angrily asked Ish-bosheth whether he was a dog's head after being accused concerning Rizpah?

speaker: Abner
quote_text: Let the young men arise and compete
prompt_context: At Gibeon, before the battle escalated, one commander proposed a contest between selected young men.
question: Who suggested that the young men arise and compete before the opposing armies?

speaker: Abner
quote_text: Turn aside from following me
prompt_context: As Asahel pursued him in battle, Abner repeatedly warned the swift young warrior to stop chasing him.
question: Who warned Asahel to turn aside and stop following him?

speaker: Abner
quote_text: Shall the sword devour forever?
prompt_context: After heavy losses at Gibeon, Abner appealed to Joab to stop the fighting before it grew worse.
question: Who asked Joab whether the sword would devour forever and urged him to call off the pursuit?

speaker: Ish-bosheth
quote_text: Why have you gone in to my father's concubine?
prompt_context: As the house of Saul weakened, Ish-bosheth confronted Abner over a matter involving Rizpah.
question: Who asked Abner why he had gone in to Saul's concubine?

speaker: Abner
quote_text: May God do so to Abner
prompt_context: Angered by Ish-bosheth's accusation, Abner vowed to transfer the kingdom to David.
question: Who swore that he would establish David's throne over Israel and Judah?

speaker: David
quote_text: You shall not see my face unless
prompt_context: When Abner sought terms with David, David made a condition involving Saul's daughter before negotiations could proceed.
question: Who told Abner that he must bring Michal before seeing his face?

speaker: Abner
quote_text: The Lord has promised David
prompt_context: While persuading the elders of Israel, Abner reminded them that God's promise favored David's rule.
question: Who told the elders of Israel that the Lord had spoken of saving his people by David?

speaker: Joab
quote_text: What have you done?
prompt_context: When Joab returned and discovered that David had received Abner peacefully, he rebuked the king and questioned Abner's motives.
question: Who confronted David for sending Abner away in peace?

speaker: David
quote_text: I and my kingdom are forever guiltless
prompt_context: After Abner was murdered, David publicly dissociated himself from the crime and pronounced a curse on Joab's house.
question: Who declared himself and his kingdom guiltless of Abner's blood?

speaker: David
quote_text: Do you not know?
prompt_context: In mourning for Abner, David emphasized to his servants the stature of the man who had fallen that day.
question: Who told his servants that a prince and a great man had fallen in Israel that day?

speaker: Rechab and Baanah
quote_text: Here is the head of Ish-bosheth
prompt_context: After murdering Saul's son, two captains brought evidence of the deed to David expecting reward.
question: Who brought Ish-bosheth's head to David and claimed the Lord had avenged the king?

speaker: David
quote_text: How much more
prompt_context: Responding to the murderers of Ish-bosheth, David compared their crime to a previous case and ordered justice.
question: Who said that wicked men had killed a righteous man in his own house on his bed?

speaker: All the Tribes of Israel
quote_text: We are your bone and flesh
prompt_context: When they came to Hebron, Israel's tribes acknowledged David's past leadership and sought to make him king over all.
question: Who told David at Hebron that he had led Israel before becoming king over them all?

speaker: Jebusites
quote_text: You will not come in here
prompt_context: When David came against Jerusalem, the city's defenders mocked him and trusted their strong position.
question: Who taunted David by saying he would not come into the city?

speaker: God
quote_text: Go up
prompt_context: When the Philistines spread out in the Valley of Rephaim, David inquired of the Lord before attacking.
question: Who told David to go up against the Philistines because they would be given into his hand?

speaker: God
quote_text: Do not go up directly
prompt_context: When the Philistines came again, David inquired once more and received a different battle plan.
question: Who told David not to go up directly against the Philistines the second time?

speaker: Michal
quote_text: How the king of Israel honored himself today
prompt_context: After David danced before the ark, Saul's daughter criticized him for what she saw as shameless behavior.
question: Who mocked David for uncovering himself before the servant girls while bringing up the ark?

speaker: Nathan
quote_text: Go, do all that is in your heart
prompt_context: When David first shared his desire to build a house for the ark, the prophet responded positively before receiving further revelation.
question: Who initially told David to go and do all that was in his heart?

speaker: God
quote_text: Would you build me a house?
prompt_context: That night, the Lord corrected David's building plan through Nathan with a larger covenant promise.
question: Who asked David, through Nathan, whether he would build a house for the Lord to dwell in?

speaker: God
quote_text: Your house and your kingdom shall be made sure
prompt_context: In the covenant promise delivered through Nathan, the Lord spoke of David's enduring dynasty and throne.
question: Who promised David that his house, kingdom, and throne would be established forever?

speaker: David
quote_text: Who am I, O Lord God?
prompt_context: After hearing the covenant promise, David sat before the Lord in humility and wonder.
question: Who responded to God's promise by asking, "Who am I, O Lord God?"

speaker: Mephibosheth
quote_text: What is your servant?
prompt_context: When shown unexpected kindness at the royal table, Jonathan's disabled son answered David with humility.
question: Who asked David why he should regard a dead dog such as himself?

speaker: Uriah
quote_text: The ark and Israel and Judah remain in booths
prompt_context: After David tried to send him home from battle, one soldier refused the comfort of his house while the army was still in the field.
question: Who told David that he would not go to his house while the ark and Israel were staying in the open field?

speaker: Joab
quote_text: If the king's anger rises
prompt_context: After Uriah died in battle, Joab instructed the messenger how to report the battle losses to David.
question: Who instructed a messenger how to report the battle losses and Uriah's death to David?

speaker: Nathan
quote_text: There were two men in a certain city
prompt_context: After David's sin with Bathsheba and Uriah, the prophet approached him with a parable about injustice.
question: Who told David the parable about a rich man taking a poor man's ewe lamb?

speaker: David
quote_text: The man who has done this deserves to die
prompt_context: Before realizing the parable applied to himself, David reacted with anger against the rich man's injustice.
question: Who declared that the man in Nathan's parable deserved to die and repay fourfold?

speaker: Nathan
quote_text: You are the man
prompt_context: After telling David a parable about a rich man and a poor man's lamb.
question: Who confronted David by saying, "You are the man"?

speaker: David
quote_text: I have sinned against the Lord
prompt_context: When the prophet exposed his guilt, the king answered with a brief confession.
question: Who confessed, "I have sinned against the Lord"?

speaker: Jonadab
quote_text: Lie down on your bed
prompt_context: When Amnon looked ill because of Tamar, his crafty cousin offered a plan to get her alone.
question: Who advised Amnon to pretend to be ill and ask for Tamar to come to him?

speaker: Tamar
quote_text: No, my brother
prompt_context: When Amnon seized her, Tamar pleaded with him not to commit such a disgraceful act.
question: Who pleaded with Amnon, saying, "No, my brother," and urging him not to violate her?

speaker: Absalom
quote_text: Has Amnon your brother been with you?
prompt_context: After Tamar came to him devastated, her brother understood what had happened and spoke with cold restraint.
question: Who asked Tamar whether Amnon had been with her and told her to keep silent for the time being?

speaker: Absalom
quote_text: Strike Amnon, then kill him
prompt_context: At the sheep-shearing feast, Absalom had already decided how to avenge Tamar and gave instructions to his servants.
question: Who ordered his servants to strike Amnon when his heart was merry with wine?

speaker: Joab
quote_text: Pretend to be a mourner
prompt_context: Wanting to move David toward restoring Absalom, Joab recruited a woman from Tekoa and coached her story.
question: Who told the wise woman of Tekoa to pretend to be a mourner before speaking to David?

speaker: Wise Woman of Tekoa
quote_text: Alas, I am a widow
prompt_context: Following Joab's instructions, a woman presented a fictional family crisis to David to draw him into a judgment.
question: Who told David a story about her two sons fighting in the field?

speaker: David
quote_text: Is the hand of Joab with you?
prompt_context: As the woman of Tekoa spoke, David perceived that someone else had put her up to it.
question: Who asked the wise woman whether Joab's hand was in the matter?

speaker: Absalom
quote_text: Why have I come from Geshur?
prompt_context: After being brought back to Jerusalem but not seeing the king, Absalom pressed Joab to arrange a meeting with David.
question: Who asked why he had come from Geshur if he still could not see the king?

speaker: Absalom
quote_text: Oh that I were judge in the land
prompt_context: While winning the hearts of Israel at the gate, Absalom posed as the one who would give justice to everyone.
question: Who said that if he were judge in the land, everyone with a case could come to him for justice?

speaker: David
quote_text: Arise, let us flee
prompt_context: When news came that Absalom had won the people's loyalty, David made a rapid decision for Jerusalem.
question: Who told his servants in Jerusalem that they must flee from Absalom?

speaker: Ittai
quote_text: Wherever my lord the king shall be
prompt_context: As David fled Jerusalem, the Gittite refused the chance to turn back and pledged himself to the king's fate.
question: Who vowed to stay with David whether for death or for life?

speaker: David
quote_text: Carry the ark back
prompt_context: As he left Jerusalem, David refused to treat the ark as a guarantee of success and entrusted himself to God's will.
question: Who told Zadok to carry the ark of God back into the city?

speaker: Shimei
quote_text: Get out, get out
prompt_context: As David fled, a Benjamite followed along cursing him and accusing him of bloodshed and usurpation.
question: Who cursed David, calling him a man of blood and worthlessness?

speaker: Abishai
quote_text: Why should this dead dog curse my lord?
prompt_context: As Shimei cursed David, one of David's men asked permission to silence him violently.
question: Who asked David for permission to go over and cut off Shimei's head?

speaker: David
quote_text: Let him curse
prompt_context: Instead of permitting retaliation against Shimei, David considered that the Lord might use the cursing for his good.
question: Who told Abishai to let Shimei curse, because the Lord might have told him to do it?

speaker: Hushai
quote_text: Long live the king
prompt_context: When David's friend came to Absalom in Jerusalem, he greeted him in a way that concealed his real loyalty.
question: Who greeted Absalom by saying, "Long live the king"?

speaker: Ahithophel
quote_text: Go in to your father's concubines
prompt_context: To strengthen Absalom's claim and harden the break with David, a counselor gave shocking political advice.
question: Who advised Absalom to go in to his father's concubines left in Jerusalem?

speaker: Ahithophel
quote_text: Let me choose twelve thousand men
prompt_context: After Absalom entered Jerusalem, one counselor urged an immediate strike against the weary and discouraged David.
question: Who proposed taking twelve thousand men to pursue David that very night?

speaker: Hushai
quote_text: This time the counsel is not good
prompt_context: Asked to evaluate Ahithophel's plan, Hushai argued for delay and painted David as too dangerous to attack hastily.
question: Who told Absalom that Ahithophel's counsel was not good this time?

speaker: Absalom and the Men of Israel
quote_text: The counsel of Hushai is better
prompt_context: After hearing both plans, the rebel court preferred the advice that would delay attack and magnify Absalom's image.
question: Who decided that Hushai's counsel was better than Ahithophel's?
option_b: Absalom and the Men of Israel
correct_answer: Absalom and the Men of Israel

speaker: David
quote_text: Deal gently for my sake
prompt_context: Before the battle in the forest, David gave a public instruction to his commanders regarding his rebel son.
question: Who ordered Joab, Abishai, and Ittai to deal gently with Absalom?

speaker: Ahimaaz
quote_text: Let me run and carry news
prompt_context: After Absalom's death, a son of the priest asked Joab to let him bring word to the king.
question: Who asked Joab for permission to run and bring news to David after the battle?

speaker: David
quote_text: O my son Absalom
prompt_context: When the king learned the outcome of the battle and the death of his son, he cried out in overwhelming grief.
question: Who lamented, "O my son Absalom, my son, my son Absalom"?

speaker: Joab
quote_text: Today you have covered with shame
prompt_context: After David's public mourning for Absalom demoralized the victors, Joab rebuked him sharply.
question: Who confronted David for grieving in a way that shamed those who had saved his life?

speaker: Shimei
quote_text: Do not let my lord hold me guilty
prompt_context: When David returned, the man who had cursed him came quickly to ask for mercy and admit his sin.
question: Who begged David not to remember how he had wronged him on the day the king left Jerusalem?

speaker: Mephibosheth
quote_text: My lord the king, my servant deceived me
prompt_context: When David questioned the crippled son of Jonathan on his absence, a competing explanation was offered against Ziba's earlier report.
question: Who told David that his servant had deceived him and slandered him to the king?

speaker: Barzillai
quote_text: How many years have I yet to live?
prompt_context: When David invited the elderly supporter to come to Jerusalem, the old man explained why he preferred to remain home.
question: Who declined David's invitation to live at court because he was too old to enjoy it?

speaker: Men of Judah
quote_text: The king is our close relative
prompt_context: After David's return, intertribal tension erupted over who had the right to escort the king back.
question: Who argued that the king was their close relative in the dispute over bringing David back?

speaker: Sheba
quote_text: We have no portion in David
prompt_context: A Benjaminite exploited the tribal tension after David's return by calling Israel to break away from the king.
question: Who cried out that Israel had no portion in David and urged the people to their tents?

speaker: Wise Woman of Abel
quote_text: Listen, listen
prompt_context: As Joab's forces besieged Abel, a woman negotiated from the wall to save her city from destruction.
question: Who spoke with Joab from the wall and arranged for Sheba's head to be thrown out?

speaker: David
quote_text: What shall I do for you?
prompt_context: During the famine, David sought terms from the Gibeonites to make atonement for Saul's past violence against them.
question: Who asked the Gibeonites what he should do to make atonement for them?

speaker: David
quote_text: The Lord is my rock
prompt_context: In his great song of deliverance, David celebrated God's rescue from all his enemies and from Saul.
question: Who sang that the Lord was his rock, fortress, and deliverer?

speaker: David
quote_text: The God of Israel has spoken
prompt_context: In his last words, David described righteous rule and explicitly named the source of the oracle he was giving.
question: Who said in his final oracle that the God of Israel had spoken?

speaker: David
quote_text: Far be it from me, O Lord
prompt_context: After three warriors fetched water from Bethlehem at great risk, David refused to drink it and poured it out to the Lord.
question: Who said the water was like the blood of men who had gone at the risk of their lives?

speaker: God
quote_text: Go, number Israel and Judah
prompt_context: In a moment of anger against Israel, a command came that set in motion David's census and its consequences.
question: Who said, "Go, number Israel and Judah"?

speaker: Joab
quote_text: Why does my lord delight in this thing?
prompt_context: When ordered to conduct the census, the commander questioned the king's desire to do it.
question: Who asked David why he delighted in ordering the census?

speaker: David
quote_text: I have sinned greatly
prompt_context: After the census was completed, David's conscience struck him and he confessed before consequences were announced.
question: Who said he had sinned greatly in what he had done by numbering the people?

speaker: Gad
quote_text: Three things I offer you
prompt_context: After David's sin in the census, the prophet came with the Lord's message presenting three judgments to choose from.
question: Who offered David three possible judgments after the census?

speaker: David
quote_text: Let us fall into the hand of the Lord
prompt_context: Forced to choose among judgments, David preferred divine mercy over human cruelty.
question: Who said he would rather fall into the hand of the Lord than into human hands?

speaker: Araunah
quote_text: Let my lord the king take and offer up
prompt_context: When David came to the threshing floor after the plague, the landowner generously offered the place and materials for sacrifice.
question: Who offered David the threshing floor, oxen, and wood for the sacrifice?

speaker: David
quote_text: I will not offer burnt offerings that cost me nothing
prompt_context: Refusing Araunah's free gift, David insisted on paying full price before building the altar.
question: Who said he would not offer to the Lord offerings that cost him nothing?
